description: Consistency-corrected phrase templates. Absolute claims downgraded where outside-control concepts may be active. Gauge and topology boundaries explicitly distinguished. Execution trace overstatement removed. All corrections grounded in same input fields as prior versions.

phrases.template: Your core system architecture is structurally mapped — visibility is established across all tracked components.
All {component_count} identifiable system components are accounted for.
System visibility is 100%. All {component_count} expected structural components are mapped.
{coverage_percent}% of identifiable system components are currently accounted for.
{coverage_percent}% of your system is structurally visible — {admissible_units} of {required_units} expected components are mapped.
The system structure is internally consistent.
Structural integrity is confirmed. {validated_units} components validated across {axis_count} reconstruction axes with {violation_count} violations.
The structural foundation is solid — all mapped components pass cross-axis validation.
No unaccounted system elements are present.
All gauge-tracked elements fall within scope — no runtime unknown-space elements detected.
{unknown_count} {element_plural} are not currently accounted for in the structural model.
{unknown_count} {record_plural} fall outside the defined structural boundary — these are not currently visible to the model.
Execution assessment has not been performed. Runtime behavior is not yet validated.
Execution layer has not run. Runtime behavior validation: NOT EVALUATED. Execution assessment is a separate evaluation — structural proof is independent.
Runtime behavior is not yet validated — execution assessment has not been performed.
No operational alerts are present.
No operational escalation conditions are active — the structural layer is clear.
{overlap_count} {component_plural} are shared across structural boundaries.
{overlap_count} cross-domain {dependency_plural} detected — critical logic spans multiple structural domains.
All components operate within clean domain boundaries — no cross-domain coordination required.
{signal_count} behavioral data {point_plural} are linked to mapped system components.
{signal_count} behavioral {signal_plural} are bound to mapped structural components — signal bindings are present in the structural model.
No behavioral signals are bound to this model — execution traces are not available.
The proven structural foundation is established at {canonical_score}. The full score of {projected_score} is achievable once execution assessment is performed.

Canonical score: {canonical_score} (CONDITIONAL). Score range: [{confidence_lower} – {confidence_upper}]. Lower bound is proven at the structural layer. Upper bound requires the execution layer to complete.
The structural proof floor is established — the achievable ceiling is unlocked by execution assessment.
All expected source data has been received — the intake boundary is complete.
Source data completeness: COMPLETE. All expected data boundaries are satisfied.
Structural patterns match the expected profile — no heuristic anomalies detected.
The score range is [{confidence_lower} – {confidence_upper}].
Score range: [{confidence_lower} – {confidence_upper}] — the floor is proven, the ceiling is conditional on execution.
{orphan_count} structural {node_plural} {have_plural} no containment relationship — isolated from the domain structure.
The system spans multiple structural domains, each with distinct functional areas and components.
Structural model spans {domain_count} domains with {surface_count} functional areas and {component_count} tracked system components.
The structural foundation is compromised — reconstruction validation has failed.
Structural integrity check: FAIL. Reconstruction validation has not passed. Review reconstruction_state.json for axis-level detail.
Parts of your system topology fall outside the classified structural boundary — {unknown_space_count} {record_plural} are unclassified.

notes.plural_placeholders: Pluralization placeholders (element_plural, record_plural, signal_plural, etc.) must be resolved by the rendering layer based on the count value. No pluralization logic is defined in this config.
notes.placeholder_resolution: All placeholders must be resolved from GAUGE data fields at render time. No default or fallback values may be used. If a placeholder cannot be resolved, the phrase must not be rendered.
notes.implication_rewrites: Phrases rewritten to implication form in GAUGE.MEANING.LAYER.EXECUTIVE.IMPACT.02. Original factual templates preserved in git history. Deactivated redundant variants remain in this file with status: inactive for audit traceability.
notes.redundancy_reduction: One active phrase per concept. Shared scope preferred; CTO used where no shared variant exists. Inactive phrases retain their phrase_id and original template for auditability.
notes.consistency_corrections: GAUGE.MEANING.LAYER.EXECUTIVE.CONSISTENCY.CORRECTION.01: PHRASE-01-SHARED absolute visibility claim downgraded; PHRASE-04-CTO scoped explicitly to gauge runtime boundary (not topology classified boundary); PHRASE-10-CTO execution trace claim removed — structural signal binding only.
